title: Relationship frontmatter
_comment: Schema for relationship primitives. A relationship is a directed link between two entities (people, companies, projects) with a typed kind and a strength signal. Downstream consumers (graph builder, second-brain-mapping, future relationship-rebuild aggregator) depend on these fields. Malformed YAML drops the edge from the graph. Includes the cross-type frontmatter contract (provenance, confidence, freshness_days, last_verified, source_count, memory_class, entity_ids) shared across all 8 typed memory primitives. memory_class default for relationship is procedural. entity_ids cross-source linking (slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear).

source_entity.description: The entity the relationship originates from (person, company, or project name, or wikilink target).

target_entity.description: The entity the relationship points to.

relationship_kind.description: Typed kind of relationship. Use 'other' only when none of the named kinds fit.

memory_class.description: Episodic captures one-time events; procedural captures repeatable, time-stable knowledge. Default for relationship is procedural.

entity_ids.description: Cross-source identifiers. Keys: slack, github_pr, github_issue, jira, notion, gmail, linear. Values: source-system IDs.
